architecture/ipc.title: WebSocket IPC
architecture/ipc.description: The IPC server bridges the TypeScript core to native clients -- the Swift menu bar app and the Tauri dashboard. It runs on ws4001/shell and provides real-time state broadcasting plus a request/response action protocol.

architecture/module-bus.title: ModuleBus
architecture/module-bus.description: The ModuleBus is Fort's central nervous system. Every module publishes and subscribes to typed events through this single bus. There are no direct imports between peer modules -- events are the only communication channel.

architecture/overview.title: Architecture Overview
architecture/overview.description: Fort is a polyglot AI agent platform built as a monorepo with four packages:

architecture/task-graph.title: TaskGraph
architecture/task-graph.description: The core invariant of Fort: every conversation creates a task. Tasks are the atomic unit of transparency. If Fort is doing work, there is a task tracking it. If there is no task, no work is happening.

cli/agents.title: fort agents
cli/agents.description: Manage core and specialist agents in the agent registry.

cli/behaviors.title: fort behaviors
cli/behaviors.description: Manage behavior rules that shape how agents respond. Behaviors are context-scoped rules injected into agent prompts at runtime.

cli/doctor.title: fort doctor
cli/doctor.description: Run health checks across all Fort modules and report their status.

cli/flags.title: fort flags
cli/flags.description: Manage feature flags for safe, incremental rollouts. Flags support bake periods, spec linkage, and promotion/rollback workflows.

cli/harness.title: fort harness
cli/harness.description: Run goal-driven self-improvement cycles. The harness proposes changes, runs them through approval, and manages rollback if needed.

cli/index.title: CLI Reference
cli/index.description: The Fort CLI is the primary interface for managing your Fort agent platform.

cli/introspect.title: fort introspect
cli/introspect.description: Inspect Fort's internal module graph, capabilities, and event system. Useful for debugging and understanding system structure.

cli/llm.title: fort llm
cli/llm.description: Configure LLM providers and send prompts with optional memory context.

cli/memory.title: fort memory
cli/memory.description: Query, search, and export the memory graph. Fort stores knowledge as nodes and edges in a SQLite-backed graph with optional MemU sidecar integration.

cli/plugins.title: fort plugins
cli/plugins.description: Load, unload, and manage plugins that extend Fort's capabilities.

cli/rewind.title: fort rewind
cli/rewind.description: Snapshot and restore system state. Rewind captures configuration, memory, and module state so you can safely experiment and roll back.

cli/routines.title: fort routines
cli/routines.description: Create and manage scheduled routines. Routines are repeatable multi-step workflows that run on a cron schedule or on demand.

cli/schedule.title: fort schedule
cli/schedule.description: View all scheduled routines and event triggers in one place.

cli/status.title: fort status
cli/status.description: Display a system overview including agents, tasks, memory, tools, and scheduler state.

cli/tasks.title: fort tasks
cli/tasks.description: Inspect the task graph. Every operation in Fort creates a task, making this the primary audit trail.

cli/threads.title: fort threads
cli/threads.description: Manage conversation threads. Threads are long-lived conversations that can span multiple tasks and agents.

cli/tokens.title: fort tokens
cli/tokens.description: Monitor token usage across agents and models, and configure spending budgets.

cli/tools.title: fort tools
cli/tools.description: Browse and search the tool registry. The tool registry enforces the reuse-before-build principle -- always check here before creating new capabilities.

getting-started/authentication.title: Authentication
getting-started/authentication.description: Fort uses Claude as its LLM backend. There are several ways to authenticate.

getting-started/first-steps.title: First Steps
getting-started/first-steps.description: With Fort installed and authenticated, run through these commands to verify everything works.

guides/agents.title: Agents
guides/agents.description: Fort runs on a registry of core agents and specialist agents. Core agents handle orchestration, memory, scheduling, and reflection. Specialist agents are purpose-built for specific domains and hatched on demand.

guides/behaviors.title: Behaviors
guides/behaviors.description: Behaviors are rules injected into LLM prompts to shape how Fort's agents respond. They act as persistent instructions — once set, a behavior applies automatically whenever its context matches.

guides/feature-flags.title: Feature Flags
guides/feature-flags.description: SQLite-backed feature flags with bake periods and automatic rollback

guides/flows.title: Flows
guides/flows.description: Flows are deterministic workflows defined in YAML. Unlike free-form LLM conversations, flows execute a fixed sequence of steps with predictable behavior, branching logic, and error handling. Use flows when you need repeatable, auditable processes.

guides/integrations.title: Integrations
guides/integrations.description: Gmail, Calendar, iMessage, Brave Search, and Browser integrations

guides/llm.title: LLM Integration
guides/llm.description: Fort routes every LLM call through a three-tier model system with automatic complexity-based routing, context enrichment, and streaming support.

guides/macos-native.title: macOS Native
guides/macos-native.description: Swift menu bar app, native notifications, and macOS system integration

guides/memory.title: Memory
guides/memory.description: Fort stores knowledge in a graph-based memory system backed by SQLite. The graph consists of typed nodes connected by edges, enabling rich relational queries across everything Fort learns.

guides/permissions.title: Permissions
guides/permissions.description: Tiered action model for controlling what Fort can do autonomously

guides/plugins.title: Plugins
guides/plugins.description: Plugin system with security scanning and sandboxed execution

guides/rewind.title: Rewind
guides/rewind.description: Snapshot-based backup and restore for Fort state

guides/routines.title: Routines
guides/routines.description: Routines are scheduled tasks that run automatically on a cron schedule. They are the backbone of Fort's proactive behavior — morning summaries, inbox triage, end-of-day reviews, and periodic maintenance all run as routines.

guides/self-coding.title: Self-Coding
guides/self-coding.description: Harness self-coding cycle for autonomous feature development

guides/threads.title: Threads
guides/threads.description: Threads are Fort's conversation primitive. Every interaction — whether a quick question or a multi-day project — lives in a thread. Threads are backed by the TaskGraph, giving full traceability from conversation to execution.

guides/tokens-and-budgets.title: Tokens & Budgets
guides/tokens-and-budgets.description: Fort tracks every token consumed across all LLM calls and provides budget controls to prevent runaway costs. Token data is stored in SQLite alongside the rest of Fort's operational data.

guides/tools.title: Tools
guides/tools.description: The Tool Registry is Fort's mechanism for enforcing reuse-before-build. Every capability — sending email, running tests, searching code — is a registered tool. Before building anything new, agents and developers must search the registry to check if an existing tool handles the need.

intro.title: Welcome to Fort
intro.description: Fort is a self-improving personal AI agent platform. It combines long-lived specialist agents, graph-based memory, deterministic workflows, and macOS native integration into a single local-first system.
